content.full_text:
April 27, 2005

Kevin Mullin
KM2 Communications
90 South Spruce Avenue, Suite M
South San Francisco, CA 94080

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. W-05-067

Dear Mr. Mullin:

You have asked for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).1 Specifically, you wished to know if a conflict of interest would exist if you resigned from your position as a member of the Workforce Investment Board (“WIB”) of San Mateo County and later submitted a competitive bid for a future project to that same body.

Please note that the Commission does not provide advice relating to past conduct and can only advise prospectively. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), enclosed.) We have enclosed copies of the following Commission documents for your review: “Can I Vote? Conflicts of Interest Overview” and “Revolving Door and Other Post-Employment Issues.” Also your facts suggest that there is the possible application of Government Code section 1090, which generally provides that an officer or employee of any governmental agency may not make a contract in which the officer or employee has a financial interest. Generally, any contract made in violation of section 1090 is void and cannot be enforced.

The Commission is not permitted to offer advice on bodies of law outside the confines of the Act, and we therefore strongly recommend that you consult the Attorney General's office regarding the possible application of section 1090 to any contract you may consider entering with the county.

1 Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures²
ER:rd
I:\AdviceLtrs\05-067

² "Can I Vote" and "Revolving Door" fact sheets
Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A)
